page: Amazon Web Services g7.8xlarge is a graphics-intensive server instance belonging to the g7 family. It is powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and features an x86_64 architecture. The compute configuration consists of 32 dedicated physical Intel cores running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz, with one thread per core. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 4.0 GB per core.

For acceleration, the g7.8xlarge includes a single NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 GPU with 32 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is provided via a 930 GB NVMe SSD, offering high-speed local read and write capabilities. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 85 Gbps, facilitating rapid data transfer for distributed workloads.

This hardware profile represents a specialized resource allocation, balancing high-frequency dedicated Intel CPU cores with professional-grade GPU acceleration and fast local NVMe storage. The absence of hyperthreading ensures predictable single-threaded performance per core. The instance is designed for graphics-intensive workloads, professional visualization, 3D rendering, and GPU-accelerated compute tasks that require high network throughput and fast local storage.
description: Amazon Web Services g7.8xlarge is a graphics-intensive server instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical Intel x86_64 cores running at 3.9 GHz with a single thread per core, paired with 128.0 GB of system memory. For hardware acceleration, the instance includes one NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 GPU with 32 GB of VRAM. Storage is supported by a 930 GB local NVMe SSD, and networking capabilities reach a baseline bandwidth of 85 Gbps. This configuration delivers a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of memory per core alongside dedicated graphics processing. The g7.8xlarge is engineered for workloads such as professional visualization, 3D rendering, and GPU-accelerated compute tasks that benefit from high-speed local storage and high network throughput.
